channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado, Michael Crowley, Anton Troianovski and Pranav Baskar

item.title: No Longer Off Limits, the Strait of Hormuz Remains Thorny Politically
item.description: Even after a cease-fire, Iran is keeping a chokehold on traffic, forcing countries to cut deals that could put them at odds with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard that appeared over the weekend in Tehran shows Iranian soldiers with American military planes and ships caught in a net, with the message “The Strait of Hormuz will stay closed.”

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: Israeli strikes against Hezbollah in Lebanon threatened the cease-fire between the United States and Iran ahead of scheduled peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Katie Reilly
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of an airstrike in the Tallet el-Khayat area of Beirut, Lebanon, on Thursday.

item.title: What We Know About the U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Talks in Pakistan
item.description: Vice President JD Vance will lead the United States delegation which is set to meet with Iranian officials in Pakistan on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Aamir Qureshi/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The Foreign Ministry offices in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Thursday. Pakistan will host talks between the United States and Iran.

item.title: Amid Trump’s Threats, NATO Labors to Survive the Iran War
item.description: President Trump is citing the unwillingness of European nations to back the United States in the conflict as another reason to scale back or abandon the alliance. And he still wants Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Mark Rutte, the secretary general of NATO, in the Netherlands in June. The two met again in Washington this week, as the war in Iran has deepened the gulf between Mr. Trump and the alliance.

item.title: Netanyahu Says He Ordered Israel to Hold Talks With Lebanon About Disarming Hezbollah
item.description: But the Israeli prime minister said his country would continue its attacks against the Iran-backed armed group.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Destruction in Beirut on Thursday, a day after Israel launched an intense wave of attacks on Lebanon.

item.title: Israel Says It Will Talk With Lebanon but Gives No Sign of a Cease-Fire There
item.description: Europeans and Iran warned that the Israeli offensive against Hezbollah threatened the truce in the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Amelia Nierenberg, Christina Goldbaum and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Damage on Thursday in Beirut, which the Israeli military continued to bombard.

item.title: I.M.F. Says Iran War Will Drag Global Growth Lower
item.description: Kristalina Georgieva, the head of the International Monetary Fund, said the war in the Middle East could lead to another bout of inflation and higher interest rates.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Buildings of Shahid Beheshti University in Tehran damaged by airstrikes last week. The International Monetary Fund said on Thursday that the war would cause slower growth in the world economy this year.

item.title: Amid U.S.-Iran Negotiations, Netanyahu’s Interests May Conflict With Trump’s
item.description: As the focus shifts to negotiations between the United States and Iran, no formal Israeli participation is planned.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Marco Longari/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: At an underground bomb shelter being used as a hospital in Haifa, Israel, on Thursday, a television showed Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu addressing the nation.

item.title: Iran’s Battered Leaders Emerge From War Confident — and With New Cards
item.description: For Iran’s theocratic rulers, just surviving the U.S.-Israeli onslaught means victory. But the seeds of their next crisis may already be planted.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman waving an Iranian flag in Tehran. Iran’s leadership is entering into renewed negotiations after the cease-fire with a new set of demands.

item.title: European Leaders Demand That U.S. Cease-Fire With Iran Include Lebanon
item.description: Britain, France and the European Union condemned Israel’s strikes targeting Hezbollah in the country, saying these threatened the truce.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bodies in a hospital morgue after Israel launched a deadly wave of attacks in Lebanon that killed more than 200 people on Wednesday.

item.title: What to Know About Israel’s Strikes on Lebanon
item.description: Israel says the truce with Iran does not cover Lebanon. But Tehran says it does and has threatened retaliation unless the bombing stops.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rescue worker on Thursday at one of the sites in Beirut, Lebanon, that Israel bombed.

item.title: Trump Again Hits Out at NATO Over Iran War
item.description: President Trump lashed out after hosting Mark Rutte, NATO’s secretary general, at the White House on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger and Christopher F. Schuetze
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Andrew Harnik/Getty Images

item.title: Oil Prices Edge Higher as Confidence in Cease-Fire Wavers
item.description: Investors were in a more sober mood after sharp moves on the previous day.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Lebanon Mourns After Israeli Onslaught Kills More Than 300 People
item.description: The Mediterranean nation is reeling after Israel struck more than 100 targets in 10 minutes, heightening fears that the tenuous cease-fire with Iran could unravel.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Hwaida Saad and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rescue workers at the site of an airstrike in Beirut, Lebanon, on Thursday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Christina Goldbaum, Max Bearak and Aaron Boxerman

item.title: Disagreement Over Lebanon’s Inclusion in Cease-Fire Threatens to Unravel It
item.description: The U.S. says the deal didn’t include the country, but Iran says it did. Israel is bombarding Lebanon, and Iran wants to show it supports its allies.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The skies in Lebanon’s capital, Beirut, were smoky in the aftermath of multiple airstrikes by Israel on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Finds Himself With Fragile Iran Cease-Fire After the Scramble to Make a Deal
item.description: After careening from one diplomatic extreme to another, President Trump finds himself with a fragile deal that is already showing signs of fraying.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Katie Rogers and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump’s efforts to circumvent the reality on the ground in the Middle East and move into a peace process have been hampered by an adversary that continues to hold leverage.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday.
item.description: The first day of a tentative cease-fire had the region on edge.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Is Strait of Hormuz Open Again? Maybe, but Few Ships Are Using It.
item.description: There were conflicting reports about the status of the vital shipping waterway in the cease-fire with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Shirin Hakim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Vessels off the coast of Oman on Wednesday.

item.title: Vance Says Lebanon Was Never Part of U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Deal
item.description: The vice president sought to downplay Israel’s continued bombardment of Lebanon, which he insisted had “nothing to do with” Iran.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Jonathan Ernst
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance speaking to reporters in Budapest, on Wednesday.

item.title: White House Knew About Pakistan’s Cease-Fire Post on X Before It Was Sent
item.description: Pakistan’s prime minister posted a public plea on X for President Trump to extend his Tuesday evening deadline for Iran. The White House was directly involved in shaping the message.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan at the White House to meet President Trump in September.

item.title: Has Trump Met His War Goals in Iran?
item.description: On the first day of the pause, Iran fired missiles and launched drones in the region. It said an oil refinery on Lavan Island had been attacked. Israel continued its strikes in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump announced Wednesday he would move forward with diplomacy during a tenuous two-week cease-fire with Iran.

item.title: Uncertainty Clouds Cease-Fire as Lebanon and Gulf States Are Struck
item.description: A day after a pause in fighting was announced between the United States and Iran, many questions remained, including the status of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Yeganeh Torbati, Euan Ward and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians celebrating the cease-fire in Tehran on Wednesday.

item.title: As Trump Swings on Iran War, Republican Leaders in Congress Are Largely Silent
item.description: In a week in which President Trump has veered from threatening to wipe out Iranian civilization to declaring a cease-fire, Congress is out of session and lawmakers with the power to declare war are mostly in the dark.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Inside the Capitol last month, as lawmakers left Washington for a two-week break.

item.title: Fed Minutes Show Officials in No Rush to Cut as Iran War Scrambled Outlook
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has left the Federal Reserve braced for higher inflation, with more officials open to the possibility of rate increases.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jerome H. Powell, the Federal Reserve chair.

item.title: Oil Prices Fall, but Energy Firms Remain Frozen After U.S.-Iran Deal
item.description: Analysts said oil and natural gas energy companies would not quickly restore production unless attacks stopped and ships started moving through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
item.media:description: Tankers anchored last month off Muscat, Oman, near the Strait of Hormuz. Until shipping traffic starts moving again, oil and gas companies are not going to reopen the taps.

item.title: For Trump, Resuming Combat in Iran Would Be Risky, Even if Cease-Fire Expires
item.description: President Trump knows that even if a cease-fire runs out with no final agreement on the issues dividing Washington and Tehran, the political risk of renewing hostilities is high.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Wednesday declared that the Iranians had been “humiliated and demoralized.”

item.title: Gulf Arab nations report a barrage of Iranian attacks since the cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Freed French Citizens Return From Iran: ‘We Were State Hostages’
item.description: Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris spent years in a Tehran prison. An Iranian court convicted them of espionage, charges that France said were baseless.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter and Ana Castelain
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tom Nicholson/Reuters
item.media:description: Jacques Paris and Cécile Kohler outside the Élysée Palace in Paris on Wednesday. Ms. Kohler said they went through “daily horror” in Evin prison, a notorious facility in Tehran.

item.title: What Is In Iran’s 10-Point Proposal, and How Does It Compare to U.S. Demands?
item.description: The plan, which reasserts Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz and maintains the country’s right to nuclear enrichment, is not the same as the one President Trump said was a “workable basis” for negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iran’s 10-point peace plan presented to American officials included reparations for the damage it has suffered in the war.

item.title: What Trump Has Accomplished in Iran After 5 Weeks of War
item.description: President Trump said that “we have already met and exceeded” his military objectives. But his goals are largely unresolved.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump went to war with Iran pledging to prevent them from getting nuclear weapons.

item.title: Hegseth Demands Iran Turn Over Uranium Stockpiles
item.description: The American defense secretary warned President Trump could still order a commando raid to seize 970 pounds of enriched uranium buried in Isfahan if Iran does not agree.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Greg Jaffe and Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth, left, and Gen. Dan Caine, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs Of Staff, held a news conference at the Pentagon on Wednesday, hours into a cease-fire between the United States and Iran.

item.title: World leaders welcome the Iran cease-fire but want the Strait of Hormuz opened soon.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pro-government demonstrators in Iran react to the announcement of the cease-fire on Wednesday.

item.title: How Pakistan Became Key Mediator Between U.S. and Iran
item.description: Pakistani officials have aggressively courted the Trump administration and made use of their longstanding ties to Iran.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan with President Trump at a summit in Egypt last year.

item.title: Cease-Fire Leaves Questions Over Fate of Iran’s Uranium
item.description: President Trump has vowed to stop Tehran from obtaining nuclear weapons. On Wednesday, he posted that the United States would work with Iran to dig up the “Nuclear ‘Dust.’”
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has said that the United States keeps Iran’s nuclear sites under “intense satellite surveillance” and would strike them again if Iran moved to extract the enriched uranium.

item.title: After Cease-Fire, Iranians Are Left to Pick Up the Pieces
item.description: Hours after a tenuous pause to a nearly six-week war, Iranians expressed relief and trepidation about the future. Some fear the government will crack down on its domestic critics.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi, Leily Nikounazar, Yeganeh Torbati and Shirin Hakim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: After Iran Cease-Fire, Ship Traffic in Strait of Hormuz Remains Throttled
item.description: A handful of vessels have crossed the crucial waterway since the U.S.-Iran truce began, but shipowners, insurers and others are wary of safe passage.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis and John Yoon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Cargo ships near the Strait of Hormuz last month. Traffic through the strait has slowed to a trickle during the war.

item.title: What to Know About the U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire
item.description: The two-week truce agreement already seems to be on shaky ground over two issues, the Strait of Hormuz and the status of Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pro-government demonstrators in Tehran early on Wednesday, after the cease-fire was announced.

item.title: War Decimated Iran’s Leadership and Pushed Up a New Generation
item.description: Since the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, authority has shifted to the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps. Younger military leaders have come to the fore.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A funeral ceremony for Alireza Tangsiri, the head of the navy for the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, and others killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes, earlier this month.

item.title: Iran Cease-Fire Does Little to Convince Netanyahu’s Critics in Israel
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had already declared victory over Iran once, in June last year. Few Israelis believe that the goals he set this time will be met, either.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Ronen Zvulun
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, facing elections before the end of October, has much riding on how Israelis perceive the outcome of the war.

item.title: Gulf Nations Face New Reality, Whether Cease-Fire Holds or Not
item.description: The countries will have to re-evaluate their relationships with Israel, Iran and the United States after a war that has exposed their vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Smoke rising from Dubai’s international airport after an attack last month.

item.title: China Pressed Iran Toward Cease-Fire, Iranian Officials Say
item.description: Beijing appeared to have helped push Tehran to accept the two-week deal with the United States, reflecting China’s growing influence and its stake in avoiding a wider war.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jeenah Moon/Reuters
item.media:description: Beijing backed Iran in vetoing a Security Council resolution to open the Strait of Hormuz, at the United Nations on Tuesday.

item.title: Israel Says Iran Cease-Fire Does Not Extend to Lebanon and Continues Strikes
item.description: Deadly airstrikes pummeled Lebanon in Israel’s largest bombing wave yet in the monthlong war with Iran-backed Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
item.media:description: Damage in a neighborhood of Beirut, Lebanon, after an Israeli strike on Sunday.
